name: courtroom_voice_id_witness_intimidation
premise: Trial judge Meredith Shaw must decide whether to allow a voice identification system to anonymize witness testimony after credible intimidation threats. Defense attorney Luis Rivera argues voice masking and AI re-voicing will impair confrontation rights and make cross-examination less effective. If Meredith denies protection, the witness may refuse to testify and the case could collapse. If she allows it, the jury may be swayed by synthetic vocal cues and the defendant’s rights may be undermined. The court must rule before jury selection resumes this afternoon.

agents.name: Meredith Shaw
agents.identity: I am the trial judge responsible for balancing witness safety with due process and ensuring the jury receives testimony without unfair distortion.
agents.goal: I want to protect a threatened witness while maintaining meaningful confrontation and avoiding any synthetic manipulation of credibility signals.

agents.name: Luis Rivera
agents.identity: I am the defense attorney responsible for preserving confrontation rights and preventing technological protections from becoming hidden prosecution advantages.
agents.goal: I want the system excluded or narrowed, because justice requires the defendant be able to test the witness’s account in an undistorted form.

choices: I will allow anonymized, re-voiced testimony with strict limits and a jury instruction, accepting some confrontation tradeoff to secure testimony and safety.
I will deny AI re-voicing and instead use physical protections and closed-circuit viewing, accepting risk of witness noncooperation to preserve confrontation integrity.
forced_choice.call_to_action: Jury selection resumes this afternoon and a threatened witness refuses open testimony. Meredith Shaw must choose between allowing AI voice anonymization or relying on non-synthetic protections. What does Meredith Shaw do?
